---
title: "cowichan_climate"
author: "JL"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(here)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
theme_set(theme_classic(base_size = 18)) #for ggplot
```

```{r}
climate <- read.csv(here("clean_data", "CGOP_climatevalues.csv"))
climate <- climate %>% 
  mutate(year = as.character(year))
# scaling the climate variables
climate_scale <- climate %>% 
  select(1, c(ends_with("tot.precip"), ends_with(".temp")))

j <- sapply(climate_scale, is.numeric)
climate_scale[j] <- scale(climate_scale[j])
climate_scale <- climate_scale %>% 
    mutate_if(is.numeric, round, 3)
remove(j)


climate_long <- climate_scale %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -year, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(season = str_extract(variable, "^[^.]+"),
    measurement = case_when(
      str_detect(variable, "precip") ~ "precip",
      str_detect(variable, "min\\.temp") ~ "min temp",
      str_detect(variable, "max\\.temp") ~ "max temp",
      str_detect(variable, "mean\\.temp") ~ "mean temp")) %>%
  select(year, season, measurement, value)%>%
  mutate(season = factor(season,
                         levels = c("spring", "summer", "fall", "winter")))

real <- climate %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -year, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(season = str_extract(variable, "^[^.]+"),
    measurement = case_when(
      str_detect(variable, "precip") ~ "precip",
      str_detect(variable, "min\\.temp") ~ "min temp",
      str_detect(variable, "max\\.temp") ~ "max temp",
      str_detect(variable, "mean\\.temp") ~ "mean temp")) %>%
  select(year, season, measurement, value)%>%
  mutate(season = factor(season,
                         levels = c("spring", "summer", "fall", "winter")))
```

```{r}
(scal <- climate_long %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value, group = interaction(season, measurement), color = measurement))+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey80") +
  geom_line()+  
  theme_bw(base_size = 18) +
  facet_wrap(~season)+
   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
             strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white"))+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "grey40", "deepskyblue", "blue4"))+
  labs(x = "", y = "Scaled climate", color = ""))

ggsave(plot = scal, "C:/Users/Jenna/OneDrive - The University Of British Columbia/Data Projects/CowichanIPMs/Figures/climate/scaled.line.png", width = 10, height = 8)
```

```{r historical climate}
# read in data from ClimateNA (long term averages)
longterm <- read.csv("C:/Users/Jenna/Dropbox/Williams' Lab/Cowichan IDE/LoggerData/ClimateNA/ClimateNA_CGOP_ClimateData.csv")
#the variables I want here are:
## Tave_sp	spring mean temperature (°C)
##  	Tmax_sp	spring mean maximum temperature (°C)
## Tmin_sp	spring mean minimum temperature (°C)
##  	PPT_sp	spring precipitation (mm)
## MAP	mean annual precipitation (mm)
## they define spring as MArch, April, and May
longterm <- longterm %>% 
  dplyr::select(period, Tave_sp, Tmax_sp, Tmin_sp, PPT_sp, MAP, Tave_sm, Tmax_sm, Tmin_sm, PPT_sm,Tave_wt, Tmax_wt, Tmin_wt, PPT_wt, Tave_at, Tmax_at, Tmin_at, PPT_at)

PPT_sp <- longterm$PPT_sp[[1]]

(precip <- real %>% filter(measurement == "precip") %>% 
    ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value))+
  geom_col(fill = wes_palettes$Darjeeling2[2], color = "black")+
  labs(y = "Total precipitation (mm)", x = "Year")+
  #geom_hline(yintercept = PPT_sp, linetype='dashed')+
  #annotate("text", x = "2024", y = PPT_sp, label = "1961-1990 Spring Precip", vjust = -0.5)+
  #scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0), n.breaks = 15)+
  theme_bw(base_size = 18) +
  facet_wrap(~season)+
   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
             strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white")))

(temp <- real %>% filter(measurement != "precip") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value, group = measurement, color = measurement)) + 
  geom_line()+
  labs(y = "Temperature (C)", x = "Year", color = "")+
  theme(legend.position="none")+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "grey40", "deepskyblue")) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 18) +
  facet_wrap(~season)+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
             strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white")))

(raw_climate <- temp+precip+ plot_layout(guides = "collect") & theme(legend.position = 'bottom'))

ggsave(plot = raw_climate, "C:/Users/Jenna/OneDrive - The University Of British Columbia/Data Projects/CowichanIPMs/Figures/climate/raw values.png", width = 15, height = 8)

```

```{r histograms}

(hist <- ggplot(climate_long, aes(x = value, fill = measurement)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, color = "black")+
  facet_grid(season ~ measurement, scales = "free") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 18) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("red", "grey40", "deepskyblue", wes_palettes$Darjeeling2[2]))+
  labs(x = "standardized climate", fill = ""))
ggsave(plot = hist, "C:/Users/Jenna/OneDrive - The University Of British Columbia/Data Projects/CowichanIPMs/Figures/climate/histogram.png", width = 8, height = 8)

```
